# Multiplication → Multiply-Subtract → 32x16

*Auto-generated from CEVA-XC4500 Vec-C Intrinsics documentation*

---

Main table → Multiplication → Multiply-Subtract → 32x16

32x16

32x16
Performs multiply-subtract 32x16 operation. The first source is 32-bit wide and the second
source is 16-bit wide. The products are accumulated with the 40-bit destination.
Available Switches
        Number of atomic operations. An atomic operation is defined as a single multiply-
        subtract 32x16 operation.Used for two vector destinations, while the first vector
Hhop
        destination is always fully written, the second vector destination number of atomic
        operation is set by Ohop. 9op œ Hhop œ 16op
        Number of atomic operations. An atomic operation is defined as a single multiply-
Oop
        subtract 32x16 operation. 1op ≤ Oop ≤ 8op
SHX     SHX is a pseudo-switch which is replaced by either >>8 or <
const   When used, vy[Y]p is constant throughout the operations.
vmpsX Selects the VMSNX and VPSX set within modv0 or modv2 mode registers.

Intrinsic     vmsulw_v32_v32_v32_v40_v40_SHFL16_const[_p]
name
Spec syntax   vmsulw {Hhop ,SHFR8|SHFL16 [,const][,vmpsX]} vx[0], vy[0], vv[V]p, voz1[0], voz2[0], ?vprX

Return type   vec40_t

              1    HHOP          uint8     9..16           Number of atomic operations
              2    IN1_V32       vec_t                     Input vector operand
              3    IN2_V32       vec_t                     Input vector operand
              4    IN3_V32       vec_t                     Input vector operand

Operands      5    IN3_OFST      uint8     0..7            Offset for the first DW used from operand
                                                           4

              6    IN3_PART      uint8     LOW,HIGH        Word part which is used for operand 4
              7    IN4_V40       vec40_t                   Output vector operand
              8    RES2_V40      vec40_t                   Output vector result operand
              9    IN_VPR        vprex_t                   Vector predicate operand
              vec_t vIn;
              vec_t vIn2;
              vec_t vIn3;
              vec40_t vRes1;
C example     vec40_t vRes2;
              vprex_t vecPred;
              ...
              vRes1 = vmsulw_v32_v32_v32_v40_v40_SHFL16_const_p (16, vIn, vIn2, vIn3, 0, LOW, vRes1, vRes2,
              vecPred);

                   IN_VPR last operand is relevant only for
              1.
                   vmsulw_v32_v32_v32_v40_v40_SHFL16_const_p version.
Comments           This intrinsic returns two results. The first result variable should be placed to

2.   the left of the = sign (lvalue). The second result variable should be passed as
                   an additional parameter (RES2_V40).

                   an additional parameter (RES2_V40).

Main table → Multiplication → Multiply-Subtract → 32x16
Intrinsic     vmsulw_v32_v32_c32_v40_v40_SHFL16[_p]
name
Spec syntax   vmsulw {Hhop ,SHFR8|SHFL16 [,vmpsX]} vx[0], vy[0], vcVp, voz1[0], voz2[0], ?vprX

Return type   vec40_t

              1    HHOP          uint8     9..16           Number of atomic operations
              2    IN1_V32       vec_t                     Input vector operand
              3    IN2_V32       vec_t                     Input vector operand
              4    IN3_C32       coef_t                    Coefficient operand
Operands
              5    IN3_PART      uint8     LOW,HIGH        Word part which is used for operand 4
              6    IN4_V40       vec40_t                   Output vector operand
              7    RES2_V40      vec40_t                   Output vector result operand
              8    IN_VPR        vprex_t                   Vector predicate operand
              vec_t vIn;
              vec_t vIn2;
              vec40_t vRes1;
              vec40_t vRes2;
C example     coef_t vcoefIn;
              vprex_t vecPred;
              ...
              vRes1 = vmsulw_v32_v32_c32_v40_v40_SHFL16_p (16, vIn, vIn2, vcoefIn, LOW, vRes1, vRes2,
              vecPred);

                   IN_VPR last operand is relevant only for
              1.
                   vmsulw_v32_v32_c32_v40_v40_SHFL16_p version.
Comments           This intrinsic returns two results. The first result variable should be placed to
              2.   the left of the = sign (lvalue). The second result variable should be passed as
                   an additional parameter (RES2_V40).
